refnamediv.refpurpose: Inspect the platform-authentication state and produce attestation

refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: trustctl inspects the platform-authentication state maintained by platformd-trustd.service8, evaluates its policies, and produces attestation. It is a client of the io.platformd.Trust varlink7 interface; the reading commands require no privilege, while attestation requires access to the TPM and provisioning requires privilege to write the boot reference.

listitem.para: Show the boot evidence and a summary of the platform-authentication state.
List the tracked login sessions with their lock and verification state.
Show the record for the session ID, including the identity behind it and whether that identity is verified.

Evaluate POLICY for the session ID and print the result together with the reason for it. The policies are fresh-user-verification (the session is active, unlocked and was verified within the freshness window) and local-trusted-session (in addition, the identity is verified and the boot is verified-local). The result is policy-satisfied or denied.
Show the recorded authentication events, most recent first.
Show the measured-boot PCRs of the SHA-256 bank and the boot grade.
Show the runtime authentication measurement log: the authentication and lock events folded into the extend-only TPM NV index, with the baseline and current values needed to replay it.

Produce a TPM quote over the boot PCRs, bound to NONCE (a hexadecimal string). If none is given a random nonce is generated. The quote is verifiable against the attestation key's public part without trusting the service.
Produce a complete attestation token in the Entity Attestation Token format, bundling the quote, the boot measurements and the runtime log.
Show the enrollment evidence: the Endorsement Key public key and, when present, its manufacturer certificate, together with the Attestation Key public key and TPM name.

Complete a credential-activation challenge, given the credential blob and encrypted secret produced by a verifier's TPM2_MakeCredential (both hexadecimal). The challenge is recovered only if the attestation key and the Endorsement Key are resident on this TPM, which proves the attestation key genuine.

Compute the expected boot measurement from the sections of the unified kernel image UKI, using systemd-measure1, and record it as the boot reference. This is normally run by the kernel-install8 plugin on each kernel installation.
Print a short help text and exit.

refsect1.title: Exit status
refsect1.para: On success 0 is returned, a non-zero failure code otherwise.

listitem.para: The directory holding the io.platformd.Trust socket. Defaults to /run/platformd-trustd.
